stemHtml: A system called active clearance control is fitted on many modern turbofan engines. How does this system work?

options.a: Compressor blades are heated by APU bleed air to reduce the gap between the blade tip and the outer casing.
options.b: It cools the outer casing of the turbine section to minimise the gap between the turbine blade tips and the outer casing.
options.c: The outer casing of the turbine section is heated to allow for more cooling airflow to the turbine blades.
options.d: It cools the individual turbine blades from inside to make sure they do not scrape the outer casing.
